name: Opacus (Differentially Private PyTorch Training)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Meta AI / PyTorch team

performance.notes: DP-SGD adds per-sample gradient clipping overhead — 2-5x slower than non-private

agent_readiness.notes: Differential privacy for PyTorch model training via DP-SGD. from opacus import PrivacyEngine. optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters()). privacy_engine = PrivacyEngine(). model, optimizer, data_loader = privacy_engine.make_private_with_epsilon(module=model, optimizer=optimizer, data_loader=loader, epochs=10, target_epsilon=50, target_delta=1e-5, max_grad_norm=1.0). # train loop as normal. epsilon = privacy_engine.get_epsilon(delta=1e-5). Accountant: RDP, Gaussian. Not all layers supported: avoid BatchNorm (use GroupNorm). Poisson sampling for train loader. max_grad_norm is privacy budget per sample.

gotchas: BatchNorm incompatible with DP — use GroupNorm or LayerNorm instead
DataLoader must use Poisson sampling: poisson_sampling=True
2-5x training slowdown due to per-sample gradient clipping
Privacy budget epsilon requires careful choice — lower is better but hurts accuracy
Not all PyTorch layers/models supported — check compatibility
best_when: Training ML models on sensitive data (healthcare, finance) with DP guarantees
Regulatory compliance requiring formal privacy guarantees (GDPR, HIPAA)
Research on privacy-preserving ML with quantified epsilon/delta
avoid_when: Non-sensitive data where privacy isn't a requirement
When model accuracy is critical and DP accuracy degradation is unacceptable
Very small models or datasets where DP overhead dominates
summary: Opacus trains PyTorch models with differential privacy via DP-SGD. PrivacyEngine().make_private_with_epsilon(model, optimizer, loader, target_epsilon=50, target_delta=1e-5). train loop unchanged. get_epsilon() for budget tracking. RDP accountant. Apache-2.0 by Meta AI. Best for DP ML on healthcare/finance data. 2-5x slowdown. BatchNorm incompatible (use GroupNorm). Epsilon selection requires calibration.
